ucs.title: [LLM] TA459 weaponized RTF/Office document spawning VBS or PowerShell loader (ZeroT / PlugX / NetTraveler chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic TA459 spearphishing chain in which a weaponized Office/RTF lure (frequently exploiting CVE-2017-0199 / CVE-2012-0158) targeting Russian, Belarusian, or Mongolian recipients spawns wscript/cscript/mshta or PowerShell, which then drops ZeroT, NetTraveler, or PlugX. The combination of Office-parent → script-host child → outbound HTTP fetch is highly specific to TA459's documented operations against CIS/Eurasian governmental and military targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_id) as pid values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_id as pid | join type=left dest pid [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443,53) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as dest process_id as pid] | where isnotnull(c2_ip) | eval risk=case(match(process,"(?i)(IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadFile|FromBase64String|-enc|-EncodedCommand|hidden|-w 1|wscript.shell|http[s]?://)"),"high", true(),"medium") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process c2_ip c2_port app risk | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let scriptChildren = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let suspect = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (scriptChildren)
| extend lure = tolower(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId = tostring(ProcessId), InitiatingProcessSHA256;
let egress = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemotePort in (80,443,8080,8443,53)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (scriptChildren)
| project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, NetProcId = tostring(InitiatingProcessId), RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessFileName;
suspect
| join kind=inner (egress) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.NetProcId
| where NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 10m)
| extend SuspectCmd = iif(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadFile|FromBase64String|-enc\s|-EncodedCommand|-w\s+1|hidden|wscript\.shell|http[s]?://)", true, false)
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where AttachmentCount > 0
    | where SenderFromDomain !endswith "<your-corp-domain>"
    | project EmailTime = Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, SenderIPv4, Subject, NetworkMessageId
) on $left.AccountName == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where EmailTime between (Timestamp - 2h .. Timestamp)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, SenderFromAddress, Subject, NetworkMessageId, SuspectCmd, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: TA459 (G0062, suspected PRC-nexus, overlapping with activity Proofpoint tracked alongside KeyBoy/Goblin Panda tooling) almost exclusively delivers payloads via weaponized RTF/Office attachments that exploit Equation Editor or HTA/CVE-2017-0199 to spawn a script host (wscript/mshta) which fetches a second-stage VBS that loads ZeroT, NetTraveler, or PlugX. Generic Office-spawn-script rules fire too
